english: evidently a present-form of the pf. bčir-ba (bcir ba), to press, to squeeze.

I. acc. to grammatical analogy 1. vb. n. to gčud-pa (gcud pa), to be twisted, distorted, pf. °čus ('chus). — 2. sbst. curvature, crookedness, distortion. — 3. adj., more frq. °čus-pa ('chus pa) crooked, wry, k'a-°čus (kha 'chus) Wdn. the mouth being wry, distorted Lt.; also obstinately perverse; fig. yig-°čus (yig 'chus) Med. frq., prob. = k'am-lóg (kham log). II. pf. bčus (bcus), fut. bču (bcu), imp. čus (cus), W. *ču-če* (*chu ce*), 1. to lade or scoop (water), ču-mig-la ču (chu mig la chu) to draw water from a well Dzl.; ču-tóm (chu tom) water-conduit Sch. — 2. to irrigate, to water, žin (zhing) a field Cs. (?) III. nán-gyis °ču-ba-la (nan gyis 'chu ba la) Tar. 127, 6, when he was pressed hard, was urged with importunity; (this signification, however, seems to rest only on this passage).
to be mistaken Pth., v. °p'yúg-pa ('phyug pa).

evidently vb. n. to °jún-pa ('jun pa), hence 1. to be tamed, subdued, made to yield, stóbs-kyis (stobs kyis) by force, lás-kyis (las kyis) by hard work. — 2. to confess Cs. — 3. to wrap or twist Sch. — 4. to fix Sch. — 5. to fix one's self Sch.; °čál-sar °čun ('chal sar 'chun) entangled in vicious indulgences Sch.
1. to wish, to long for Lex. — 2. to shrink Cs.

pf. bčes (bces), °čes ('ches) (Sch.), fut. bče (bce), imp. čes (ces), 1. to assure, to promise, k'as °če-ba (khas 'che ba) Lex., resp. žál-gyis °če-ba (zhal gyis 'che ba) id. — 2. resp. for smrá-ba (smra ba), like γsúṅ-ba (gsung ba) (?)

also °čág(s)-pa ('chag(s) pa), pf. bšags (bshags), fut. bšag (bshag), imp. šog (shog), W. *šág-če* (*shag ce*), 1. to cleave, to split, šin (shing) wood; sóg-les °čég-pa (sog les 'cheg pa) to saw Sch.; °čeg-byéd ('cheg byed) (a thing) that cleaves, a hatchet Cs. — 2. to confess, to acknowledge; v. also bšág-pa (bshag pa) and šóg-pa (shog pa).
an incorr. form of čád-pa (chad pa) or mčéd-pa (mched pa).
pf. bčems (bcems), fut. bčem (bcem), to chew Med.
Cs. 1. to believe, give credit to; blo-°čél-ba (blo 'chel ba) (?) col. id. — 2. Lexx. = žén-pa (zhen pa) to wish (?).
1. = °čám-pa ('cham pa) Glr. and Lexx. — 2. vb. n. to jóms-pa ('joms pa) 4 W., *da čoms soṅ* (*da choms song*) now it is done.

I. vb. n., pf. šor (shor), 1. to escape, slip, steal away; to drop from, stón-mo šór-gyis (ston mo shor gyis) as the meal escaped him, as he was deprived of the meal Dzl.; rtsa-krág °čór-ba (rtsa khrag 'chor ba) hemorrhage, bloody flux Med.; bkrag-°čór (bkrag 'chor) without splendour, lustre-less; nor °čór (nor 'chor) the money is gone, spent, lost Thgy.; sdóm-pa °čór (sdom pa 'chor) the duty is violated Glr.; mé-la, čú-la °čór-ba (me la, chu la 'chor ba) to be consumed by fire, carried off by water; *čaṅ mi tuṅ dé-nẹ k'a mi šor* (*chang mi tung de ne kha mi shor*) W. I will not drink any beer, then the mouth cannot run away, i. e. then no indiscreet words will escape my mouth; to flow out, to run, of a leaking vessel, to run over, of a full one. — 2. to come out, to break out, frq. of fire; °krúg-pa šor ('khrug pa shor) a quarrel, a war broke out, also of water breaking through an embankment etc. — 3. to go over, to pass, from one person or thing to another, rgyál-sa Bód-nas Me-nyág-la šor (rgyal sa bod nas me nyag la shor) the supreme power passed from Tibet to Tanggút Glr.; γžán-gyi dbáṅ-du šor (gzhan gyi dbang du shor) then I shall get into the power of another Mil.; rkún-ma-la šor (rkun ma la shor) it became the prey of a thief. — 4. W. to run away, flee, escape, elope, inst. of °brós-pa ('bros pa), *šór-te ča-dug* (*shor te cha dug*) he retires, falls back. II. vb. a., pf. (b)šor ((b)shor), fut. γšor (gshor) (?) 1. to pursue, chase, hunt after, ri-boṅ rgyas (ri bong rgyas) hares by means of nets; nya °čór-ba (nya 'chor ba) to fish Dzl.; Cs. also to strain (?); °čor-sgég ('chor sgeg) a seducer; a swaggerer Sch. (cf. sgég-pa (sgeg pa)). — 2. to light, kindle, set on fire (?)

1. disorderly, dissolute, immoral. — 2. disorderly action or conduct, dissoluteness, °čól-pa sna-tsógs spyód-pa ('chol pa sna tshogs spyod pa) committing several acts of immorality Wdn. — kro-bo-°čól-pa (khro bo 'chol pa) n. of a demon. (Cf. °čól-ba ('chol ba) II).
